zone_6b_notes: Plant bare-root crowns in early spring (late March to April) in Farmington. June-bearing varieties produce one large crop over 2–3 weeks in late May to June — the most flavorful type, ideal for fresh eating, jam, and freezing. Matted row system: plant 18 inches apart, allow runners to fill in to 12-inch-wide rows. Remove flower buds the first year to allow plants to establish. Mulch heavily with straw in winter (3–4 inches) after hard freeze. Renovate beds after harvest each year: mow to 4 inches, thin to 4–5 plants per foot, fertilize. Earliglow, Allstar, and Jewel are standard reliable varieties for Zone 6b Missouri.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant bare-root crowns late March to April

water: consistent — 1–1.5 inches/week; drip irrigation keeps fruit dry and reduces disease
